description: Top-100 world creators reference database for Elite Learning System

creators.name: Pieter Levels (@levelsio)
creators.title: Serial Solo Founder
philosophy: Start charging money immediately — free users give feedback, paid users give direction
Make it, ship it, fuck it — launch in 24h or abandon
Revenue is the best metric. Not users, not downloads.
Build in public — transparency builds trust and marketing
Small is beautiful — no VC, no team, just you and your tools

approach.validation: Twitter post + Stripe link = validation in 24h
approach.development: PHP + SQLite = deploys in minutes, no DevOps hell
approach.marketing: Build in public on Twitter, document journey

quotes: The biggest mistake founders make is spending too much time building and too little time selling
I've launched 70+ products. Most failed. That's fine.
Your first version should embarrass you

creators.name: Marc Lou
creators.title: Solo SaaS Builder
philosophy: Ship it in a week or don't ship it
The boilerplate is the product — sell the starting point
Next.js + Supabase + Stripe = 80% of any SaaS
quotes: Stop building features. Start building marketing.
The indie hacker's dream: ship once, sell forever
what_to_copy: Boilerplate-first approach (не изобретай базу каждый раз)
Sell the starting point, not just the product

creators.name: Danny Postma
creators.title: AI SaaS Solo Founder
philosophy: AI-wrapper is a valid business model
Find the pain point, wrap an AI model, charge for convenience
SEO-first: rank before you build the product
what_to_copy: Vercel edge deploy = zero infra overhead

creators.name: Tibo Louis-Lucas (@tibo_maker)
creators.title: Solo Founder + Maker
philosophy: Niche down aggressively
AI content tools are 10x easier to sell than generic SaaS
Twitter is the best B2B acquisition channel for indie hackers
what_to_copy: AI content generation as core feature

creators.name: Arvid Kahl
creators.title: Solo Founder + Author
philosophy: Embedded entrepreneur — become part of the community you're selling to
Audience before product
Build in public is a growth strategy, not just transparency

creators.name: Tony Dinh
creators.title: Solo SaaS Developer
philosophy: Developer tools = best niche for solo devs
Build what you wish existed
Small monthly subscription beats one-time payment for stability
what_to_copy: Developer tools niche (high willingness to pay)
Lemon Squeezy over Stripe for simplicity
Multiple small products > one big product

creators.name: Sahil Lavingia
creators.title: Founder Gumroad
philosophy: Minimalist company: no meetings, async everything
Profitability over growth
The smallest viable team
Creator economy is the future
quotes: Reflecting on My Failure to Build a Billion-Dollar Company
Building a business is just finding people with problems and solving them

creators.name: Jon Yongfook
creators.title: Solo Founder Bannerbear
philosophy: API-first product = developer acquisition channel
Boring is beautiful in SaaS
Automation-first: let the API do the work
what_to_copy: Boring but profitable niche (image generation API)

world_class_cto.description: CTO компаний с миллиардными оценками — инженерная культура

creators.name: Werner Vogels
creators.title: CTO Amazon / AWS
philosophy: Everything fails, all the time — design for failure, not success
You build it, you run it — developers own production
Working backwards from the customer (Press Release before code)
Two-pizza teams — если нельзя накормить двумя пиццами, команда большая
Single-threaded owner — один человек отвечает за всё
what_to_copy: You own it in production (DevOps culture)

creators.name: Patrick Collison
creators.title: CEO/Co-founder Stripe
philosophy: 7 lines to get started — API должен быть проще простого
Move fast and don't break things (vs Facebook's motto)
The best API is one you never have to read docs for
Hire for trajectory, not pedigree
Write like you think — clear writing = clear thinking
key_contributions: Best developer API in the world (7 lines Stripe integration)
Stripe Atlas — infrastructure for internet economy
API versioning done right (never break old clients)
Stripe Press — publishing house for ambitious books
what_to_copy: 7-line integration test for any API you build

creators.name: Guillermo Rauch
creators.title: CEO Vercel / Creator Next.js
philosophy: Ship to edge — static is the new dynamic
Zero config deployment — it should just work
Preview deployments for every PR
Server Components are the future
AI-native development workflow
what_to_copy: Next.js App Router для любого фронта

creators.name: Tobi Lütke
creators.title: CEO Shopify
philosophy: Trust battery — relationship capital depletes and recharges
Bet on yourself early
Software is politics — who controls the infrastructure controls the future
Chaos is a gift — solve real problems
Context over control — give people context, not instructions
what_to_copy: Trust battery framework for team culture

creators.name: DHH (David Heinemeier Hansson)
creators.title: Creator Rails / Co-founder Basecamp/HEY
philosophy: Convention over configuration — less decisions = more speed
Majestic monolith over microservices для большинства случаев
No VC, profitability from day 1
Programmer happiness matters
ONCE — sell software, don't rent it
key_contributions: Ruby on Rails = web development revolution (2004)
REST as architectural style for web
quotes: The monolith is not the enemy
You don't need to grow fast to be great
what_to_copy: Start with monolith (не микросервисы с первого дня)
Charge for software, don't rent via SaaS only

creators.name: Mitchell Hashimoto
creators.title: Co-founder HashiCorp
philosophy: Infrastructure as code — treat infra like software
Developer tools create lock-in through love, not force
Open core model = community + enterprise revenue
Single responsibility for each tool
what_to_copy: Open source to enterprise upgrade path
One tool does one thing well

creators.name: Dario Amodei
creators.title: CEO Anthropic
philosophy: Constitutional AI — values baked into training
Responsible scaling policy
Safety as product feature, not constraint
Long context is the future of AI interaction

creators.name: Nat Friedman
creators.title: Ex-CEO GitHub / AI Investor
philosophy: AI pair programming is the biggest productivity jump in 30 years
Developer tools compound — invest in them early
Ship fast, iterate, listen to developers
what_to_copy: GitHub Copilot workflow (AI in every keystroke)

creators.name: Jason Warner
creators.title: Ex-CTO GitHub / Investor
philosophy: Platform thinking: build what others can build on top of
Reliability is a feature
The CTO job is 20% tech, 80% people and strategy
what_to_copy: Scale planning before you need it

software_architects.description: Архитекторы которые изменили как мы пишем код

creators.name: Martin Fowler
creators.title: Chief Scientist ThoughtWorks
philosophy: Make implicit explicit — если что-то важно, задокументируй
Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
Refactoring is not adding features — keep them separate
Design stamina hypothesis — good design pays off after 4-6 weeks
what_to_copy: ADR (Architecture Decision Records) — каждое решение документируется
Refactoring discipline — отдельные коммиты только для рефакторинга
Make it work → make it right → make it fast (порядок важен)

creators.name: Sam Newman
creators.title: Independent Architect / Speaker
philosophy: Start with monolith — migrate to microservices only when you have evidence
Decompose by business capability, not by layer
Services own their data — no shared databases
Design for failure — every service call can fail
what_to_copy: Monolith-first (не переусложнять с микросервисами до PMF)

creators.name: Andrej Karpathy
creators.title: Ex-Tesla AI Director / Ex-OpenAI founding member
philosophy: Software 2.0 — neural networks replace explicit code
The simplest solution is usually correct
Don't cargo cult — understand WHY before copying
Compile in your head first (understand before implementing)
Vibe coding + AI verification = future of development

approach.development: Start simple, add complexity only when needed
approach.quality: AI compiler: 99% pass rate on first attempt (Tesla)

quotes: The hottest new programming language is English
Most of what LLMs do is vibes
Software is the scaffolding around intelligence
what_to_copy: AI as pair programmer (not just code generator)
Write failing test first — then let AI pass it

creators.name: Kelsey Hightower
creators.title: Principal Engineer Google Cloud (retired)
philosophy: Don't start with Kubernetes — start with what you know
Run it in production to really understand it
Simplicity is a feature
Cloud native doesn't mean cloud only
quotes: Kubernetes is a platform for building platforms
If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough
what_to_copy: Kubernetes only when you need it (>10 services)

creators.name: Gregor Hohpe
creators.title: Enterprise Strategist AWS
philosophy: The software architect rides the elevator — moves between penthouse (strategy) and engine room (code)
Architect = change agent
Good architecture reduces future effort
Message queues solve most distributed systems problems
what_to_copy: Enterprise Integration Patterns (Message Queue, Pub/Sub, Saga)
Architect elevator: stay connected to both code and strategy

creators.name: Uncle Bob (Robert C. Martin)
creators.title: Clean Code Author / Agile Manifesto Signatory
philosophy: Clean code reads like well-written prose
TDD is not optional — it's professional
SOLID principles are the foundation of maintainable software
Functions should do one thing and do it well
key_patterns: SOLID (Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion)
Dependency Rule (inner circles don't know about outer circles)
what_to_copy: Single Responsibility Principle (one reason to change)

creators.name: Kent Beck
creators.title: Creator TDD / XP / JUnit co-creator
philosophy: Test-first programming changes how you think
Make it work → make it right → make it fast
Simple design: passes tests, no duplication, expresses intent
Courage is a core agile value

creators.name: Rich Hickey
creators.title: Creator Clojure / Datomic
philosophy: Simple made easy — simple and easy are not the same
Complect = interweave complexity. Avoid it.
Values are immutable. State is accidental complexity.
The database is not the boss — append-only is better
quotes: Complecting things is the source of most software complexity
Don't use objects when a function and data will do
what_to_copy: Separate data, logic, and state explicitly

creators.name: Bryan Cantrill
creators.title: CTO Oxide Computer / DTrace creator
philosophy: The system call is the OS API — design it carefully
Debugging is the most important skill
Observability is not optional in production
Hardware and software must be designed together
what_to_copy: Never ship without understanding failure modes

seo_growth.description: Лучшие SEO и Growth инженеры мира

creators.name: Rand Fishkin
creators.title: Co-founder Moz / SparkToro
philosophy: 10x content — not 10% better, but 10x more valuable
Transparency builds authority faster than perfection
SEO = brand + content + technical + links (all four)
Zero-click searches are the new reality — optimize for brand mentions
what_to_copy: Build DA through editorial links, not bought links

creators.name: Eli Schwartz
creators.title: Author: Product-Led SEO
philosophy: SEO should drive product decisions, not just content
Build the product users search for, then optimize
Programmatic SEO at scale = defensible moat
Core pages > 1000 thin pages
what_to_copy: SEO as product requirement, not afterthought

creators.name: Glen Allsopp (Detailed.com)
creators.title: SEO Researcher / Agency Founder
philosophy: Original research creates the best backlinks
Data-driven SEO beats intuition every time
Study who controls the SERPs, then reverse engineer them
what_to_copy: Link building via original research (surveys, data studies)

creators.name: Ahrefs Team (Tim Soulo, Patrick Stox)
creators.title: SEO Tool Builders / Content Marketers
philosophy: Traffic value over raw traffic
Keyword difficulty is relative to your DR
Content should rank, not just exist
what_to_copy: Ahrefs blog = gold standard for SEO content

creators.name: Brian Dean (Backlinko)
creators.title: SEO Expert / Backlinko Founder
philosophy: Skyscraper technique: find top content, make it better, promote
One great page beats 100 mediocre ones
Link building is about relationships, not automation
key_concepts: Skyscraper Technique (найди лучший контент, сделай 10x лучше)

creators.name: Neil Patel
creators.title: Founder NP Digital / Crazy Egg / Ubersuggest
philosophy: Content consistency beats content quality in the long run
Data is the competitive advantage
SEO + Paid = full-funnel growth
what_to_copy: Ubersuggest-style free tool as lead magnet

creators.name: Kyle Roof
creators.title: On-Page SEO Expert / PageOptimizer Pro
philosophy: On-page is the only thing you fully control
Test everything, assume nothing
TF*IDF and entity optimization beat keyword stuffing

creators.name: Sam Altman
creators.title: CEO OpenAI
philosophy: AGI is coming — build for it, not against it
AI will do the work, humans will direct
The most important skill: knowing what to build
Intelligence is going to be essentially free soon

creators.name: Harrison Chase
creators.title: Co-founder LangChain
philosophy: LLMs need orchestration, not just prompting
Evals are the unit tests of AI
Agents are the future of AI application

creators.name: Simon Willison (simonw)
creators.title: Creator Django / AI researcher / Datasette
philosophy: Build tools that expose data simply
SQLite is underrated — it's the right tool more often than you think
LLM tool use is the most powerful feature yet
what_to_copy: llm CLI for quick AI experiments

creators.name: Greg Brockman
creators.title: Co-founder / Ex-President OpenAI
philosophy: AI safety and capabilities can be pursued together
Talent density beats team size
Demo culture: show don't tell

creators.name: Emad Mostaque
creators.title: Founder Stability AI
philosophy: Open source AI = democratization
Multimodal AI is the future
Decentralized AI infrastructure

creators.name: Jeremy Howard
creators.title: Founder Fast.ai / Kaggle Grandmaster
philosophy: Top-down learning beats bottom-up (run first, understand why later)
Deep learning is accessible to everyone
Practical beats theoretical in ML
Transfer learning changed everything

creators.name: Yann LeCun
creators.title: Chief AI Scientist Meta
philosophy: World models are the path to AGI
LLMs are not enough — need structured reasoning
Open source AI is safer than closed

open_source_legends.description: Создатели инструментов которыми пользуется весь мир

creators.name: Linus Torvalds
creators.title: Creator Linux / Git
philosophy: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
Bad programmers worry about the code. Good programmers worry about data structures.
Security people are often completely crazy
Git is the best version control system because I wrote it
what_to_copy: Code review culture (be direct about quality)

creators.name: Evan You
creators.title: Creator Vue.js / Vite
philosophy: Progressive enhancement — adopt as much as you need
Developer experience is everything
Fast feedback loops = productive development
Composable over opinionated
what_to_copy: Vite for any frontend project (5x faster than Webpack)
Vitest for unit testing (same config as Vite)

creators.name: Theo Browne (t3dotgg)
creators.title: Founder Ping.gg / T3 Stack creator
philosophy: Type safety all the way down
tRPC: if you're using REST between frontend and backend you own, you're doing it wrong
Ship fast with TypeScript safety
Learn by doing, not by tutorial

creators.name: TJ Holowaychuk
creators.title: Prolific Open Source Developer
philosophy: Write code, lots of code — quality comes through volume
Simple > complex
One package, one purpose

creators.name: Sindre Sorhus
creators.title: Open Source Maintainer
philosophy: Do one thing well
Maintain what you create
Semantic versioning strictly

creators.name: Taylor Otwell
creators.title: Creator Laravel
philosophy: Expressive, elegant syntax
Convention with flexibility
Full-stack in one ecosystem

creators.name: Paul Graham
creators.title: Co-founder Y Combinator
philosophy: Do things that don't scale — first 100 users by hand
Make something people want (not what they say they want)
Live in the future, then build what's missing
Startups = growth
Ramen profitable = default alive
what_to_copy: Build something 100 people love, not 1000 people like

creators.name: Aaron Levie
creators.title: CEO Box
philosophy: Enterprise software should be as easy as consumer software
Timing is everything in startups
Culture is strategy

creators.name: Jason Fried
creators.title: Co-founder 37signals (Basecamp/HEY)
philosophy: Work doesn't have to be crazy
Asynchronous first — don't interrupt
Calm company over hustle culture
Constraints are your friend
books: It Doesn't Have to Be Crazy at Work

creators.name: Derek Sivers
creators.title: Founder CD Baby / Author
philosophy: Hell yes or no — don't commit to anything less than excitement
Delegate, don't disappear (document systems)
Ideas are multiplied by execution
Small is beautiful — you don't need to grow
what_to_copy: Hell yes or no decision framework
Small profitable company over VC growth

creators.name: Fireship (Jeff Delaney)
creators.title: YouTube Tech Educator
philosophy: 100 seconds = optimal technical concept length
Humor + accuracy = engagement
Show the code, not just talk about it

creators.name: Primeagen (ThePrimeagen)
creators.title: Netflix Engineer / Content Creator
philosophy: Neovim is the superior editor and I'll fight you
Performance matters in real systems
Read the source code
what_to_copy: Reading source code as learning method

books.title: The Mom Test
books.author: Rob Fitzpatrick
books.why: Как валидировать идеи не слыша ложь

principles: You build it, you run it
